system.description:
Through discipline, intuition, and an ever-deepening communion with the flame, you have learned to channel its supernatural power—not as a servant, but as a lord.

The Fire Lord spell list is composed of the following spell schools : law, fear, fire

Cantrip

You know two cantrips of your choice from the Fire Lord spell list. You learn a third cantrip when you reach 4th level, and a fourth when you reach 8th level. You can replace a cantrip when you gain a level.

Spell Points

The Fire Lord table shows how many spell points you have. To cast one of your Fire Lord spells of 1st-level or higher, you must spend a number of spell points dependent on the spell’s level, shown on the table below. You can also cast a spell you know at a higher spell level by spending the appropriate number of spell points. The maximum spell level you can cast is shown in the Spell Level column of the Fire Lord table. You regain all expended spell points when you finish a short or long rest.

Table: Spell Cost

Spell Level

Spell Points

1st

2

2nd

3

3rd

5

4th

6

5th

7

Spells known of 1st level and higher

You know two 1st-level spells of your choice from the Fire Lord spell list.

The Spells Known column of the Fire Lord table shows when you learn more fire lord spells of your choice. Each of these spells must be of a level that you are able to cast. For instance, when you reach 3rd level in this class, you can learn one new spell of 1st- or 2nd-level.

Additionally, when you gain a level in this class, you can choose one of the fire lord spells you know and replace it with another spell from the fire lord spell list, which also must be of a level of which you can cast.

Spellcasting Ability

Constitution is your spellcasting ability for your Fire Lord spells, since their power is derived from the fiery power of your flesh. You use your Constitution whenever a spell refers to your spellcasting ability. In addition, you use your Constitution modifier when setting the saving throw DC for a Fire Lord spell you cast and when making an attack roll with one.

Spell save DC = 8 + your proficiency bonus + your Constitution ability modifier

Spell attack modifier = your proficiency bonus + your Constitution ability modifier

Spellcasting Focus

You can use an arcane focus as a spellcasting focus for your Fire Lord spells.
